headline: Americas Gold & Silver DRILLS 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu — Launches Largest-Ever 64,000m Campaign at Galena
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) discovered 10 new high-grade silver-copper-antimony and silver-lead veins at the Galena Complex — including 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu over 1.3m — and is backing it with a company-record 64,000m drill campaign across Galena and Cosalá.

headline: DENISON MINES Clears Final Hurdle — CNSC Approves Wheeler River Construction, Canada's First ISR Uranium Mine in 20+ Years
snippet: Denison Mines has received final regulatory approval from Canada's Nuclear Safety Commission to construct its Wheeler River uranium project in Saskatchewan — the country's first new large-scale uranium mine approved in over 20 years and the first ever to use in-situ recovery (ISR) mining.

headline: GOLD RETREATS to $4,743 — Still Up $1,568 Year-Over-Year After Record Run Near $5,600 in Q1
snippet: Gold traded at $4,743/oz on April 9, pulling back $59 from the prior session after a dramatic Q1 that pushed the metal to a record near $5,600/oz. Year-over-year gains still exceed $1,500 — a historically unusual pace of appreciation.

headline: Silver UP 150% Year-Over-Year — Outpacing Gold at Decade Highs, $77 Target In Sight
snippet: Silver has surged more than 150% year-over-year to decade-high levels, outperforming even gold's record-breaking run. Analysts project prices reaching $77.04/oz by mid-April as industrial demand — including data centre buildout — compounds monetary buying.

headline: Copper Holds Near Records But LME INVENTORY Swells to 8-Year High — Is Demand Already Rolling Over?
snippet: Copper futures steadied around $5.75/lb after hitting record highs on supply disruption and tariff fears, but LME warehouse stockpiles have climbed to an eight-year high — a potential demand-weakness signal that could check further upside despite bullish structural narratives.

headline: Energy Storage Boom Flips Lithium Outlook — Morgan Stanley Forecasts 80,000t DEFICIT in 2026 vs. Prior Surplus
snippet: Battery storage demand is accelerating a turnaround for the oversupplied lithium market. Morgan Stanley now forecasts an 80,000t LCE deficit in 2026 — a sharp reversal from an expected 61,000t surplus in 2025 — as producers like Core Lithium restart idled capacity.

headline: Mining M&A HITS 13-Year High — $93.7B in 2025 Deals, Gold Accounting for 77% of Q1 2026 Volume
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $93.7 billion in 2025 — the highest yearly total since 2012 and up 27% from 2023. The pace has continued into 2026, with 85 transactions totalling over $11 billion recorded in January alone, 77% concentrated in gold and silver assets.

headline: Zijin Mining Makes $4.05B APPROACH on Canada's Allied Gold — Chinese Capital Targets North American Deposits
snippet: China's Zijin Mining has made a $4.05 billion acquisition approach toward Canada's Allied Gold Corp, the latest example of international capital deployment targeting North American gold assets as mining mega-deals rise 68% from 2024 levels.

headline: Gold Fields SIGNALS More Deals — CEO Open to Further M&A as Senior Miners Consolidate
snippet: Gold Fields confirmed it remains open to further acquisitions as it looks to grow through M&A at the right price, adding a major senior-tier buyer to an already active dealmaking environment across the gold sector in 2026.

headline: Dryden Gold Hits THREE New High-Grade Discoveries at Gold Rock — Sparrow and Ruby Zones Step Out 300m
snippet: Dryden Gold Corp. has identified three new high-grade gold discoveries on its Big Master Gold System at Gold Rock, with the Sparrow and Ruby zones representing 300-metre step-outs to the northeast from previously known high-grade mineralization.

headline: Uranium STAGED Dramatic Q1 Rally, Then Iran War Shock Triggered Selloff — Long-Term Contracts Still at Records
snippet: Uranium spot prices rallied dramatically through Q1 2026 before geopolitical shocks tied to the Iran conflict triggered a sharp selloff. The divergence is stark: spot sentiment volatile, long-term contracting demand at record highs, and supply constraints still deepening.

headline: Analyst Issues $6,900 GOLD TARGET — Central Bank Buying at Elevated Pace, Dollar Weakness Drive Bull Case
snippet: A new Wealth Megatrends report evaluates the case for a $6,900/oz gold price under its bull scenario, citing persistent central bank accumulation at elevated pace, dollar weakness, and sustained safe-haven allocation as the primary drivers of the ongoing structural bull market.

headline: Buy Newmont BEFORE Q1 Earnings — Up 19% YTD, Analysts Say Valuation Still Hasn't Caught Gold's Move
snippet: Newmont has climbed roughly 19% year-to-date yet analysts argue the stock remains undervalued relative to gold's performance — making Q1 earnings a potential catalyst for a further re-rating and the reason at least one outlet is calling it a buy right now.

headline: Gold Mining Stocks: WINNERS and LOSERS at the Start of Q2 — Who's Finally Catching Up to Bullion?
snippet: As Q2 2026 opens, gold mining equities are showing stark divergence — some names finally closing the valuation gap with bullion while others continue to lag. The analysis identifies which miners are winning the catch-up trade and which remain dead weight.
